tool.name: WASP2 Make Reads
tool.description: Generate reads with swapped alleles for WASP remapping

param.label: Include indels
param.help: Process indels in addition to SNPs. Default is SNPs only.

param.label: Maximum indel length
param.help: Maximum indel length to process (bp). Longer indels are skipped.

param.label: Maximum alternate sequences
param.help: Maximum alternate sequences per read. Reads with more variants are skipped.

param.label: Insertion quality score
param.help: Quality score (Phred) for inserted bases in alternate reads

tool.help:
**WASP2 Make Reads**
Generates alternate reads with swapped alleles for the WASP remapping pipeline. This is step 1 of the WASP bias-correction workflow.
**WASP Pipeline Overview**
1. **Make Reads** (this tool): Generate reads with swapped alleles 2. **Remap**: Align the generated FASTQ files with your aligner (BWA, STAR, etc.) 3. **Filter Remapped**: Keep only reads that map to the same location
**Inputs**
- **BAM file**: Original paired-end aligned reads - **Variant file**: VCF with phased heterozygous variants - **VCF sample**: Sample name used to select heterozygous genotypes
**Outputs**
- **to_remap FASTQ**: Reads with swapped alleles to be remapped - **keep BAM**: Reads that don't overlap variants (passed through) - **to_remap BAM**: Original reads before allele swapping (needed for filter step) - **WASP data JSON**: Provenance metadata describing the generated outputs
**Workflow**
After running this tool:
1. Remap the to_remap FASTQ files with your aligner 2. Run "WASP2 Filter Remapped" with the remapped, to-remap, and keep BAM files 3. The filtered BAM can be used for unbiased allele-specific analysis
